content.problemStatement:
### **Aptitude Practice Question**

A tree increases annually by (1/8)th of its height. By how much will it increase after 2 years, if it stands today 64cm high?

---

### **Answer & Explanation**

The increase% is given by, (1/8)  × 100 % = (25/2) %

By formula,
C.I. = P (1 + (R / 100))T
⇒ C.I.= 64(1 + (25/(2  × 100)))2
⇒ C.I.= 64(9/8)2
⇒ C.I.= 64(81/64)
⇒ C.I.= 81
Total Increase = Height after 2 years−Initial Height = 81−64 = 17 cm

Therefore, the tree will compound to height 17cms after 2 years.
